failures.details: [/] Background and foreground colors do not have a sufficient contrast ratio.: Step number indicators (#d1d5db on #ffffff, ratio 1.47:1, needs 4.5:1)
[/] Background and foreground colors do not have a sufficient contrast ratio.: Connect section text (#9ca3af on #ffffff, ratio 2.53:1, needs 4.5:1)
[/privacy] Background and foreground colors do not have a sufficient contrast ratio.: Code elements in privacy page (#6b7280 on #f3f4f6, ratio 4.39:1, needs 4.5:1 — borderline fail)
[/guides] Background and foreground colors do not have a sufficient contrast ratio.: Step labels across all guide articles (#9ca3af on #ffffff, ratio 2.53:1, needs 4.5:1)
[/udemycoupons] Background and foreground colors do not have a sufficient contrast ratio.: Original price strikethrough (#979ca6 on #ffffff, ratio 2.75:1, needs 4.5:1)
[/udemycoupons] Background and foreground colors do not have a sufficient contrast ratio.: Discounted price in green (#16a34a on #ffffff, ratio 3.29:1, needs 4.5:1)
[/] Links rely on color to be distinguishable.: Link in 'Connect with GitHub' section is only distinguishable by color (blue text in gray paragraph)
[/faq] Links rely on color to be distinguishable.: Email and resource links in FAQ footer rely solely on color for distinction
[/privacy] Links rely on color to be distinguishable.: Data retention detail links in privacy page rely solely on blue color for identification
[/] Lists do not contain only `<li>` elements and script supporting elements.: An ordered list (<ol>) contains <div> elements as direct children instead of <li> elements
[/udemycoupons] Select elements do not have associated label elements.: Category filter and status filter dropdowns lack associated <label> elements. Screen readers cannot identify their purpose.
[/udemycoupons] Heading elements are not in a sequentially-descending order.: Course card titles use <h3> which may skip heading levels. Page structure jumps from h1/h2 to h3 without clear hierarchy

assumptions: All pages are public and unauthenticated
Lighthouse version 13.4.0 with axe-core 4.12 was used
Both mobile and desktop emulation were tested
Only accessibility category was audited (not performance, SEO, or best-practices)
Audits were run in headless Chrome on Linux
No form submission or login was attempted

patterns.name: gray_text_on_white_bg
patterns.description: Multiple pages use text-gray-300 (#d1d5db), text-gray-400 (#9ca3af), or text-gray-500 (#6b7280) on white backgrounds which fails WCAG 1.4.3 contrast requirements

patterns.name: green_on_white_contrast
patterns.description: Tailwind's text-green-600 (#16a34a) on white background fails WCAG AA contrast (3.29:1, needs 4.5:1)

patterns.name: links_without_underline
patterns.description: Links in text blocks across multiple pages rely solely on color (text-blue-600) for distinction, failing WCAG 1.4.1

patterns.name: unlabelled_form_controls
patterns.description: Filter dropdowns on /udemycoupons page lack associated labels, failing WCAG 4.1.2

gotchas: text-gray-300 (#d1d5db) on white has only 1.47:1 contrast ratio — one of the worst offenders
text-gray-400 (#9ca3af) on white has 2.53:1 — fails for all text sizes
text-green-600 (#16a34a) on white has 3.29:1 — common Tailwind green fails contrast
text-gray-500 (#6b7280) on gray-100 (#f3f4f6) barely fails at 4.39:1 (needs 4.5:1)
The /about page scored 100/100 — serves as a good accessibility baseline for other pages

facts.statement: All 6 pages have proper viewport meta tags without disabled zoom
All images across all pages have alt text
All pages have proper document titles
All pages have proper html lang attributes
No ARIA attribute violations were detected on any page
No keyboard tabindex or focus order issues detected
No touch target size violations detected
/about page scored 100/100 on both mobile and desktop — fully accessible
/udemycoupons page has the most accessibility issues (3 audit failures with 51 total violations)
Total of 8 unique accessibility issues across 5 of 6 pages, with 44 total occurrences

failure_modes.scenario: Color contrast failure on light text
symptoms: Gray text on white background below 4.5:1 ratio
Green text on white below 3:1 ratio
failure_modes.mitigation: Use text-gray-600 (#4b5563) or darker for body text on white. Use text-green-700 (#15803d) for green text.

symptoms: Screen readers cannot identify filter dropdown purpose
failure_modes.mitigation: Add <label> elements with for attributes or use aria-label on select elements

symptoms: Colorblind users cannot distinguish links in text
failure_modes.mitigation: Add underline decoration, bold weight, or icon indicators to links in text blocks

decisions.decision: Focus remediation on /udemycoupons first (most issues + critical severity)
Fix Tailwind color tokens globally rather than per-instance
rationale: text-gray-300/400/500 failures appear on 4 different pages
Single CSS change can fix multiple violations
Consider adding custom color tokens for accessibility

conventions: All gray text on white backgrounds must use text-gray-500 (#6b7280) or darker for WCAG AA compliance at 12px+
All links in text blocks must include underline decoration or other non-color indicators
All form controls must have associated labels (either visible <label> or aria-label)
Heading levels must not skip levels (h1 → h2 → h3, never h1 → h3)
